title: galium mollugo subsp. pycnotrichum
intro: Galium mollugo subsp. pycnotrichum, also known as the wood cranesbill or pygmy bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium mollugo plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Galium mollugo subsp. pycnotrichum are attributed to its ability to improve digestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating various ailments. Galium mollugo subsp. pycnotrichum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
